name: Reminiscing Sa'di: A Legacy of Persian Literature
description: Join us for a lecture that traces one of the most influential cultural exchanges in South Asian history — a centuries-long relationship between India and Persia that shaped the languages we speak, the literature we read, and the ways power itself was imagined.About the LectureFor centuries, Persian was not a foreign language in India.It was the language of courts and administration, poetry and philosophy, diplomacy and social mobility. Long before English occupied that role, Persian connected vast parts of the subcontinent to a broader Persianate world stretching from Anatolia to Central Asia.Its influence was so deep that generations of Indian scholars, poets, bureaucrats, and intellectuals began their education through Persian. Communities such as Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Khatris built careers through their mastery of the language, while poets from Iran travelled to India seeking patronage, literary fame, and intellectual companionship.This lecture explores the remarkable journey of Persian in India — not simply as an imported language, but as a tradition transformed by the people who adopted it.The session explores:Why Persian became the language of power and culture in HindustanThe lives and journeys of Persian poets who made India their homeHow Indian scholars and writers reshaped Persian literary traditionsThe ethical and political worlds contained in texts by Sa'di, Hafiz, Rumi, and othersThe influence of Persian on Hindi, Urdu, and other Indian vernacularsHow Persian civilisation retained its distinct identity across centuries of political and religious changeMoving between history, literature, politics, and storytelling, the lecture reveals a world in which ideas, languages, and people moved across borders far more freely than we often imagine today.At its heart, this is a story about exchange: how cultures borrow, adapt, and reinvent one another — and how those encounters continue to echo in our everyday lives.About the SpeakersDr. Mukhtar Ahmed teaches at the Department of Persian, University of Delhi, where his research focuses on Modern Persian Literature and Indo-Persian literary traditions. His forthcoming book, From Persia to Paris: A Journey of Ideas and Cultures, examines the intellectual and literary exchanges between Persian and French traditions. Known among friends for both his scholarship and his cooking, he remains deeply interested in the movement of ideas across languages and civilisations.Heena Goswami is a public policy professional with an academic background in history and has recently submitted her PhD dissertation at the University of Delhi. Born and raised in Rajasthan, her interests span history, politics, technology, and public life. An avid reader and former educator, she now spends much of her time encouraging others to read, often through stories about books and ideas shared online.Your ticket includes a 300/- coupon redeemable on F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: ALT EFF Film Club x GuruJal Gurugram | 19th June
description: Join us for a screening of Miles Away; a visual and auditory immersion of the lives of migrant, female brick labors. This film is intimate, cinematic and sensory.Part of our 2024 Program, this was Juror Dia Mirza's reaction:"I was very moved by 'Miles Away' as for the first time I saw the perspective of what Climate Change is doing to women. It was very special to see so many women coming together to tell the story and feeling at ease as they shared personal moments with the female filmmakers. I felt as if I was a part of their lives."To become a Film Club Member, continue with the regular booking process. The membership option will appear after you select the venue and date.All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Exclusive screenings of the world's best environmental cinemaAccess to a community of environmentally inclined, film-loving peoplePost-film discussions.Student Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening).CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFIf you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 12 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation. With snacks. Always snacks.

name: Daughters of Shahjahan at Red fort (A Heritage walk)
description: Daughters of Shahjahan Heritage walk at the Redfort (With Dramatic Reading of play excerpts)Join Darwesh Heritage Walks & Sabhyata Foundation for a unique Immersive experience at the Red Fort .The Red Fort (Lal Qila), an iconic symbol of India's history, stands proudly in Old Delhi as a silent witness to centuries of political, architectural, and cultural change. Commissioned by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in 1638, this UNESCO World Heritage Site is not just a monument—it is a storyteller, narrating tales of imperial grandeur, colonial resistance, and post-independence pride. Stories about the Mughal opulence, the power politics, the powerful place of women in our history will unfold as we walk through the bygone Mughal era.The ‘war of succession’ that took place among the four sons of Shah Jahan had one novelty, while previously, wars were fought after the death of the emperor, this war was fought when Shah Jahan was still alive. All sons and daughters of Shah Jahan participated in it. While the four sons Dara Shikoh, Shah Shuja, Aurangzeb and Murad Baksh fought against each other, the sisters Jahanara and Roshanara allied themselves with one or the other brother. Through this we will bring to you to the story of the Mughal battle of succession that changed the course of Indian history.The heritage walk includes (dramatic reading of play excerpts in Hindustani) highlighting stories and scenes from the tussle going on between Shahjahan’s Sons Aurangzeb and Dara Shikoh and the two daughters Jahanara and Roshanara Begum, each of whom sided with one brother.

name: The Listening Room
description: The Listening Room: A Space for Deep ListeningOverviewIn a world that is always talking, we rarely get the chance to truly listen. The Listening Room is a unique session dedicated to the art of being present. It is a space to share, to hear others, and to practice the kind of attention that builds real connection and empathy.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectActive Listening: Practice being fully present while others speak without the need to interrupt or give immediate advice.Shared Stories: A safe environment to share your own thoughts or experiences and feel truly heard by a small group.Quiet Reflection: Moments of silence to process what has been shared and to notice how deep listening changes our perspective.Supportive Connection: A morning at Greenr focused on human presence and the simple power of witnessing each other’s stories.Details🗓️ Date: 24th May (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:00 AM📍 Location: Greenr, Golf Course Ext Road🎟️ Contribution: ₹700

name: Kuch Kuch Queer Hai
description: Join us for a lecture that begins with a question Bollywood has spent decades avoiding: Where are all the queer rom-coms?About the LectureBollywood has given us countless love stories—epic romances, disastrous love triangles, dramatic reunions, and unforgettable meet-cutes. Yet queer love has rarely been allowed to occupy the centre of the frame.This lecture traces the curious relationship between Bollywood, queerness, and the romantic comedy. While queer movements, legal struggles, and literature have transformed how queer lives are imagined in India, cinema has often struggled to keep pace.Drawing on queer theory, genre studies, and film history, the session explores:Why the rom-com has historically excluded queer love storiesHow censorship controversies shaped representations of sexuality on screenWhat streaming platforms have changed—and what they haven'tThe political and emotional possibilities of a genuinely queer Bollywood romanceMoving between films, legal history, and cultural criticism, the lecture asks a deceptively simple question: What would happen if Bollywood finally allowed queer love to get its happily-ever-after?About the SpeakerDr. Kashish Dua is Assistant Professor of English at Jesus and Mary College, University of Delhi. A Fulbright-Nehru Doctoral Fellow, she was a visiting researcher at the University of California, Berkeley from 2023–24.Her research explores the intersections of queer literary studies, citizenship studies, and legal theory. Her work has been published by Oxford University Press, Routledge, Peter Lang, and DK London. She is also the co-editor of Vernacular Encounters: Politics and Possibilities (Routledge, 2026).An academic by day and a devoted rom-com enthusiast by night, she insists the two pursuits have far more in common than most people think.Your ticket includes a 250/- coupon redeemable on F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen’s, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. Each event is an invitation to spend an evening with an idea.

name: The Making Of A Serial Offender
description: Join us for an evening that begins with a simple question: what makes a serial offender? The answer may be more unsettling than you think.About the LectureThis lecture explores how criminal profiling moved beyond outdated assumptions about "criminal faces" to become one of the most fascinating tools in modern investigations. Drawing from forensic psychology, criminology, and real-world case studies, the session examines how investigators attempt to understand offenders through behaviour rather than appearance.Together, we'll explore:Why humans are often poor judges of who is capable of violenceThe evolution of criminal profiling, from early theories to modern investigative psychologyHow crime scene behaviour can reveal patterns of motive, planning, control, and escalationThe origins of behavioural profiling and the rise of the FBI's Behavioural Science UnitWhat famous serial offender cases reveal about fantasy, victim selection, and criminal developmentWhy serial offending remains understudied in IndiaHow forensic psychologists and investigators use Behavioural Evidence Analysis to reconstruct offender behaviourMoving beyond sensationalism and popular crime shows, this lecture examines the deeper psychological and social questions that profiling raises.Why do some individuals cross lines that most never do? What can behaviour tell us about risk, intent, and future offending? And how can India build more rigorous behavioural science frameworks for criminal investigation?Part psychology, part criminology, and part investigative science, this session offers a rare glimpse into the complex process of understanding criminal behaviour.About the SpeakerMr. Rithin Joseph is an Assistant Professor at the School of Behavioural Forensics, National Forensic Sciences University (NFSU), Delhi.Specialising in Criminology and Forensic Psychology, his work focuses on criminal profiling, deception detection, investigative psychology, and forensic behavioural analysis. He has published multiple research papers and presented over twenty-one papers at national and international conferences.Mr. Joseph has specialised training in a range of forensic investigative techniques, including Polygraph, Brain Electrical Oscillation Signature Profiling (BEOS), Suspect Detection System (SDS), Layered Voice Analysis (LVA), and EyeDetect.He currently serves as Co-Investigator on an ICSSR-funded research project examining the prediction of recidivism among first-time offenders in conflict with the law. He is also the co-author of *Crime as the Cause of Criminality* and *BRICS: Unravelling the Abyss*.A Gold Medalist in Criminology from the National Forensic Sciences University and recipient of the **Young Criminologist Award 2024**, he continues to contribute to research in criminal psychology, offender behaviour, and investigative science while mentoring postgraduate students and pursuing doctoral research in Criminal Psychology.Your ticket includes an alcoholic/non-alcoholic beverage of your choice!About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: The Art of Folding
description: The Art of FoldingOverviewA quiet practice of shaping and stillness.Using origami as a meditative, hands-on experience, participants slow down through simple folds and repetitive movement. One fold at a time.No fancy paper needed. This is a hands-on origami session using whatever you have lying around at home. Blinkit bags, newspapers, old book pages, packaging paper. If it folds, it works. Come make something with it.What is Stories of Hope?Stories of Hope is a community built around a simple idea, that people need places where they can slow down and be heard. We create small, intentional spaces for writing, listening, creating, and reflecting. There is no expectation to perform and no pressure to have the right words. Just a space to pause and be with what is already there.What to ExpectBring Your Paper: Blinkit bags, newspapers, old book pages, packaging paper, unused sheets. Anything works.Beginner-Friendly Shapes: We will work through cranes, butterflies, and doves together. No experience needed, just hands and patience.Fold Together: A quiet, unhurried session. You leave with something you made.Details🗓️ Date: 10th May (Sunday)⏰ Time: 5:30 PM📍 Location: Cha & Co, Grand View High Street🎟️ Fee: ₹700

name: Table For Ten| Food Club - Latin American Edition
description: Table for Ten is an intimate, 6-course, shared experience designed around a simple idea: that food is never just food. It holds the power to transport you to places, build memories and get you out of your comfort zone. For our next event, we’re taking you on a flavour journey at Atticus with a thoughtfully curated Latin-inspired menu, immersive games, and a little Zindagi Na Milegi Dobara magic.New cuisines can be intimidating to try alone, and this event seeks to demystify that. It is not a theme party or a screening, yet we aim to capture some of the spirit of the movie at our event.During the event, you will:have a Latin-inspired six course mealhear directly from the chef about how they built each courseengage in games and activities capturing the spirit of Zindagi Na Milegi Dobaraexchange postcards with bucket list items from someone else at the tableWho Are We: We are Diya and Sanjula, a non-profit fundraiser who spends all her money trying out the new 'it' restaurant in town, and a therapist who would have been a food critic in another life. Between us, we share a belief that food is far more than what ends up on a plate. We can be contacted on Instagram @spoonsofdelhi or on WhatsApp at +91 8448577626 for any queries.

name: A Spanish Summer Table at Paprika Tokri
description: There is something about a warm Spanish summer table that asks you to slow down.A cold sip of something bright. Small plates passed from hand to hand. The smell of saffron, garlic, olive oil, and smoked paprika in the air. Laughter carrying across the table. The ease of arriving, settling in, and letting the evening unfold.For this edition of Paprika Tokri Supper Club, we are bringing that feeling to the farm.A Spanish Summer Table is an intimate supper built around tapas-style small plates, cocktails, and a Spanish-inspired summer menu shaped by melon, peppers, citrus, almonds, saffron, wine, candlelight, and the warmth of being together.We begin with a Clarified Bloody María and small plates at the table, before moving slowly into a Spanish-inspired supper with pimentón butter roast chicken, farm pumpkin steak, saffron arroz meloso, and Spanish caramel custard to finish.This is not a rushed dinner. It is an evening to arrive, settle in, eat slowly, and share the table.Event DetailsDate: Saturday, 20 June 2026Time: 7:30 PM onwardsVenue: Paprika Tokri Farm, DelhiPrice: ₹3,500 per personSeats: LimitedMENUTo beginClarified Bloody Maríatomato water, tequila, smoked paprika, citrus, olive brineSmall platesMelon, Manchego & Mintcold melon, Manchego, mint, black pepper, olive oilCucumber Boqueronesvinegar-cured cucumber ribbons, garlic, parsley, olive oil, chilliCharred Pepper, Olive & Caper Saladroasted bell peppers, olives, capers, garlic, parsley, olive oil, vinegarPadrón-Style Green Chilliesblistered mild green chillies, olive oil, flaky saltStuffed Morels with Ajo Blancocream cheese and mushroom stuffing, chilled almond-garlic sauceKing Trumpet "Scallops"smoked paprika aioli, lemon, herbs, crispy garlicPrawn Pinchossmoked paprika, garlic, lemon, olive oilBefore the mainAgua de Valencia Spritzorange, sparkling wine, gin, citrusAt the tablePimentón Butter Roast Chickensaffron arroz meloso, citrus-garlic chicken jus, charred summer beansFarm Pumpkin Steaksaffron arroz meloso, mojo rojo, crispy chickpeas, toasted pine nuts, Manchego shavings, herbsTo finishSpanish Caramel Custarddark caramel, orange zestPlease note:This is a fixed-menu supper club. name: Before You Blame
description: Before You BlameOverviewA reflective relationship workshop about the stories we create around conflict.People explore how assumptions, emotional triggers, and personal patterns shape the way they experience conflict and relationships Understanding, before assumption.Most relationship conflicts are not just about what happened; they are about how we interpret what happened. This session focuses on exploring how our thoughts shape our connections and why friction often feels more intense than the situation warrants. "Before You Blame" is a reflective workshop designed to help you look at your assumptions and inner patterns, moving you from reactivity toward steadiness and emotional responsibility.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community-led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities, from mental well-being to personal growth, with empathy and shared human connection.What to ExpectPattern Recognition: Explore how common emotional triggers and ego-driven patterns shape conflicts in marriage, family, or work.Internal Focus: Instead of trying to fix others, we gently shift the focus inward to understand our own interpretations and responses.Practical Tools: Learn actionable ways to question stressful thoughts and practice perspective shifts using real-life scenarios.Safe Exploration: A supportive, non-judgmental environment for honest discussion. This is a space for learning and reflection, not a lecture or therapy.Key TakeawaysClarity: Gain a deeper understanding of recurring relationship dynamics.Responsiveness: Walk away with tools to help you respond thoughtfully instead of reacting impulsively.Emotional Ownership: Build the internal steadiness needed to navigate life without requiring others to change first.Details🗓️ Date: 21st June (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:00 AM📍 Location: Kanchanjunga Towers, Sector 56

location.address: A-BLOCK, KANCHANJUNGA APARTMENT, G.H.18, near Post Office, Sector 56, Gurugram, Haryana 122011, India

name: Sex, Death, And The Long War Of Life
description: Join us for a lecture that begins with a provocative proposition: You were never meant to exist. And that may be the most liberating fact about you.About the LectureHuman beings like stories.We tell ourselves that life has meaning, that history bends toward progress, that our desires are uniquely our own.Evolution tells a different story.This lecture explores the oldest conflict on Earth: the endless struggle of living things to survive, reproduce, and persist. Long before nations, religions, or political ideologies existed, life was already engaged in a ruthless competition that continues to shape every organism alive today.Drawing on evolutionary biology and psychology, the session traces how some of our deepest instincts emerged from this ancient inheritance.The lecture explores:Why evolution remains the most powerful framework for understanding lifeHow competition and cooperation coexist in the natural worldWhat sex is really doing from the perspective of evolutionWhy human beings are, like all organisms, unfinished creaturesHow morality, consciousness, and even the idea of a “soul” might be viewed through an evolutionary lensMoving from algae to human behaviour, from reproduction to mortality, the session challenges many of the comforting assumptions we hold about ourselves.About the SpeakerAmbarish Satwik is a Delhi-based vascular and endovascular surgeon, Director of the Vascular Cath Lab at Sir Ganga Ram Hospital, and Professor of Vascular Surgery at GRIPMER.Alongside his medical career, he is an acclaimed writer whose work moves across literature, history, politics, and science. His novel Perineum: Nether Parts of the Empire is a critically acclaimed exploration of sexuality and power in the British Raj.His essays, columns, and commentaries have appeared in publications including India Today, The Times of India, Outlook, Open, Tehelka, Time Out, and Hindu Business Line.Known for bringing together scientific rigour, intellectual irreverence, and dark humour, his talks often leave audiences questioning ideas they previously took for granted.Your ticket includes a complimentary drink during the session.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Journey Of Hearts - Delhi Open Mic
description: Delhi ki har gali mein ek kahaani hai.Kuch metro rides mein kho jaati hain, kuch diaries mein chhup jaati hain, aur kuch bas kisi ko sunaye jaane ka intezaar karti hain.Aaiye ek aisi shaam ka hissa baniye jahan shabdon ko manzil mile aur jazbaaton ko jagah.For The Ones Who...• Scribble thoughts in their notes app• Have poems hidden in old notebooks• Fall in love with words too easily• Miss Delhi before they've even left it• Believe stories can change peopleChahe aap perform karna chahte ho ya sirf sunna, yeh mehfil aapke liye hai.Apni kavitaayein, kahaaniyan, gaane aur dil ki baatein lekar aaiye.Kyunki Delhi mein kahaaniyon ki kami nahi, bas sunne waalon ki zarurat hai. ❤️

description: Gallery XXL & Tangerine Circle invite you to an evening of conversation, curiosity, and a treasure hunt through an art exhibition.We’ll begin by gathering over conversations before forming teams and setting off across the exhibition. Follow clues, solve puzzles, find new stories, and piece together a trail woven through the artworks on view.Think of it as a long summer afternoon spent wandering, wondering, and discovering things you didn’t know you were looking for. And for the first team to crack the hunt, there’s a prize waiting at the endDate: Sunday, June 21, 2026Time: 4 - 6 PMVenue: Gallery XXL, 2nd Floor, A-277, Block A, Defence Colony, New Delhi, Delhi 110024ABOUT TANGERINE CIRCLE:Tangerine is a literary and cultural circle for people who find meaning in stories. name: Coffee & Confessions - Alayna Cafe
description: Summer in the City is the opening chapter of CTRLlab's Summer Coffee Crawl, and serves as an intimate gathering exploring the subtle ways cafés, neighbourhood spaces, and shared rituals shape the character of a city.As the season settles into its longest evenings, join a curated community of coffee lovers, storytellers, and urban explorers for conversations on culture, connection, and place. Through thoughtfully moderated sessions, we'll reflect on the role of cafés as contemporary cultural anchors and uncover the personal stories behind the city's most cherished discoveries.Coffee Anchor examines how cafés become more than places to drink coffee—evolving into landmarks of creativity, community, and everyday belonging.Social Move invites guests to share memorable city encounters, summer rituals, and the spaces that have left a lasting imprint on their lives.Set against a summer backdrop, the evening offers a slower pace, meaningful dialogue, and the opportunity to connect with others who appreciate the nuances of urban culture.Summer Coffee Crawl 2025Stop 1 — Summer in the City14 June | Edit5, Sector 64, GurugramStop 2 — Coffee & Confessions21 June | Alayna Café, Shahpur JatStop 3 — Mode of Transport28 June | ATE Glasshouse, Lodhi RoadTickets- Single stop pass: Access to the event at that particular stop of the crawl - Three stop pass: Access to all 3 stops of the crawl

name: Raat Ke Afsane : Qutub Minar
description: Experience the magic of Qutub Minar like never before—under the stars and glowing lights. This is a guided heritage walk led by a historian, who will take you through the stunning Qutub complex after dark. As the entire site is beautifully illuminated at night, the minaret shines like Delhi’s very own Eiffel Tower—majestic, mysterious, and unforgettable.Walk through centuries of history, discover forgotten stories, and explore Delhi’s medieval past through engaging storytelling. The play of light, shadow, and silence transforms this heritage site into something truly cinematicPlaces we will cover : 1. Mughal Serai2. Alai Minar3. Iltutmish Tomb4. Qutub Minar5. Iron Pilar6. 27 Jain and hindu temples7. Alai Darwaza8. Alauddin Khilji grave and madarsa .Important Points:  1. Details of the heritage walk will be mailed to you when you book the tickets2. No guided fees for kids below 5.3. Medium of communication : Hindi and English4. The participant has the liberty to take another walk by the company against the payment they have made within 30 days since the date of the registered walk.5. The company does not have refund policy.

name: The Procrastireaders
description: The Procrastireaders: Love is Love - Pride Month ReadingOverviewA slow reading circle designed around comfort, not pressure.People come together to read, exchange thoughts, and return to books in a way that feels lighter and more approachable. Stories, shared slowly.This June, Procrastireaders is reading stories about queer love. The joy, the tenderness, the ordinary and extraordinary ways people find each other. Stories where being queer is not the conflict to be resolved but simply part of who the characters are. If that sounds like your kind of read, come join us.What is Stories of Hope?Stories of Hope is a community built around a simple idea, that people need places where they can slow down and be heard. We create small, intentional spaces for writing, listening, creating, and reflecting. There is no expectation to perform and no pressure to have the right words. Just a space to pause and be with what is already there.What to ExpectDiscussion First: We open with a conversation around the theme, the books you brought, and what drew you to them. No right answers, just an honest exchange.Silent Reading Block: We close with dedicated quiet time to actually sit with your book. Good lighting, AC, and fellow readers for company.Bring Your Own Book: Carry whatever you are currently reading or something from your "to be read" pile that needs your attention.Details🗓️ Date: 27th June (Saturday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Unlocked, 32nd Avenue🎟️ Fee: ₹700

name: SCC Gourmet Cinema - In the mood for Love
description: Step into an afternoon suspended in time.To celebrate the 25th anniversary of Wong Kar-wai’s masterpiece In the Mood for Love, Sunset Cinema Club and The Piano Man invite you to Gourmet Cinema, an intimate cinematic dining experience at The Piano Man, Eldeco Centre, New Delhi.Set in 1960s Hong Kong, In the Mood for Love is a timeless tale of longing, desire, and missed connections. With its stunning visuals, unforgettable music, and iconic performances, it remains one of cinema’s most beloved love stories.As the film unfolds, enjoy a specially curated 5-course gourmet menu, thoughtfully crafted to complement the mood and elegance of the story. More than a screening, this is an invitation to savour the beauty of what is left unsaid. Through exquisite food, evocative storytelling, and one of cinema’s most enduring love stories, immerse yourself in a world where time moves a little slower, and every moment lingers long after the credits roll.Food: Vegetarian and Non-Vegetarian options available.Drinks: 1 Alcoholic and 1 Non-Alcoholic beverage available.Please note: The menu is fixed and cannot be modified.Seating  Seating is pre-arranged. If you’ve booked a ticket, you are assured a seat. ScheduleScreening - Midnight in ParisDate: 27th June, SaturdayTime: Entry begins at 12:00 PM and the movie starts at 12.30 PMNote : The movie is in Cantonese with English subtitles.

name: 4D Polymers: 3 Dimensions & Beyond
description: Join us for a lecture that explores a future where materials don't simply exist, they respond, transform, heal, and even generate their own energy.About the LectureMost materials are passive.A bandage covers a wound. An implant supports damaged tissue. A scaffold holds cells in place.But what if materials could do more?What if they could change shape over time, respond to their environment, generate electrical signals, and actively participate in the healing process?This lecture introduces the emerging world of 4D biomaterials — materials designed not only in three dimensions, but with a fourth dimension: time.Drawing from cutting-edge research in regenerative medicine and bioengineering, the session explores how scientists are creating materials that can mimic some of the remarkable properties of living tissue. These next-generation biomaterials are capable of programmed transformations, self-powered stimulation, and dynamic interactions with the cells around them.The lecture explores:What 4D printing is and how it differs from conventional 3D printingHow materials can be designed to change shape and function over timeThe role of bioelectric signals in tissue healing and regenerationHow self-powered biomaterials can generate electrical stimulation without batteries or external energy sourcesWhy these technologies could transform the future of healthcare, implants, and regenerative medicineAlong the way, the session offers a glimpse into a future where materials are no longer passive tools but active participants in biological processes.About the SpeakerDr. Amit Nain is an Assistant Professor at the Kusuma School of Biological Sciences, IIT Delhi, where he leads research focused on developing next-generation biomaterials for tissue regeneration and advanced healthcare applications.His laboratory works at the intersection of materials science, biology, and engineering, designing bioinstructive materials that can sense, respond, and support healing processes within the body.Dr. Nain completed his B.Tech. in Biotechnology from VIT University and earned his PhD in Nano Science and Technology from National Taiwan University, where his research focused on antimicrobial nanomaterials.His work has been recognised internationally, including being named a 2024 Emerging Investigator by the Royal Society of Chemistry and a 2025 Rising Star by the American Chemical Society. He has published over 35 research articles, book chapters, and conference proceedings, and currently serves on the Early Career Board of Biomacromolecules.Outside the lab, he is a Bollywood enthusiast, poetry lover, road-trip addict, and an unapologetic fan of Friends, Breaking Bad, Homeland, and Money Heist.Your ticket includes a 200/- voucher for F&B About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Haunted Walk: Mehrauli Archaeological Park
description: Join us for a hauntingly beautiful night walk through the Mehrauli Archaeological Park, a place where centuries of Delhi’s history come alive—alongside tales of the unseen. In this special segment, Ruhaani Raatein, we delve into the world of djinns, the mystical beings believed to reside in this ancient land.Guided by a historian and storyteller, we’ll explore how djinns are viewed and worshipped in Islamic traditions, and how folklore continues to shape the spiritual character of Mehrauli even today.Our walk will take you through: The tomb of Jamali Kamali, often called the Gay Taj Mahal for its poetic mystique and timeless love story A haunting 500-year-old baoli (stepwell)The remnants of a British-era swimming pool, hidden in plain sight A peaceful lakeside café, perfect for ending the evening with a quiet reflection or a warm chaiAs night falls and shadows grow, let the stories guide you through one of Delhi’s oldest, most magical spaces—where the past is never truly past.

name: The Hidden History Of Queer Lives
description: Join us for a lecture that challenges one of the most persistent myths about queerness in India: that it is new.About the LectureHistory, as it is usually told, rarely makes room for queer lives.Queer people appear as footnotes, court cases, scandals, anthropological curiosities, or cautionary tales. Entire worlds of belonging, intimacy, resistance, and community are left out of the official record.But they were always here.This lecture traces a longer and richer history of queerness in India—one that stretches far beyond contemporary debates about identity, rights, and representation.Moving through colonial archives, legal history, community formations, and political movements, the session explores:What existed before Section 377—and what colonial rule sought to eraseHow communities such as hijras, kothis, aravanis, and jogatis built their own social worldsThe long struggle for queer rights in IndiaWhy remembering queer histories changes how we understand the presentAnchored by the anthology Queer India Now, this lecture is ultimately about memory itself: who gets remembered, who gets forgotten, and what becomes possible when lost histories are recovered.About the SpeakersDhamini Ratnam is National Culture Editor at Hindustan Times and an award-winning journalist specialising in gender and sexuality. Over a seventeen-year career spanning publications such as Mint, Mumbai Mirror, and Mid-Day, she has written extensively about LGBTQIA+ lives in India and broader questions of social change.Dhrubo Jyoti is a Delhi-based writer and editor whose work focuses on questions of caste, queerness, and representation. Their work seeks to foreground voices and histories that have often remained at the margins of mainstream LGBTQIA+ narratives.Your ticket includes a 250/- coupon redemable on F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen’s, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. Each event is an invitation to spend an evening with an idea.

name: Night Heritage walk :  Humayun's Tomb & Museum
description: Step into the grandeur of Humayun's Tomb with us for an immersive Night heritage walk that goes beyond stone and symmetry.Curated by Darwesh Heritage Walks in collaboration with Sabhyata Foundation.We will not only explore the monument’s magnificent architecture, its Persian influences, and its place in the evolution of Mughal design — but also rediscover Humayun himself, perhaps the most underestimated of the Mughals. His struggles, exile, return, and legacy shaped the empire in ways we rarely pause to notice."Driven from his throne, yet never from his destiny's embrace,Humayun's return laid the path on which Akbar would leave his trace"Our journey will then continue to the Humayun's Tomb Museum — because no visit to the tomb is truly complete without experiencing the stories, artefacts, and narratives preserved there.Come, walk through history — and meet the emperor behind the monument.

name: From mehfils to movies: Courtesans of India
description: A Heritage Baithak at Qudsia BaghWhat happens when history remembers a woman only through the lens of morality and forgets her art, intellect, and influence?Join us for a fascinating journey into the world of India's courtesans—Nagarvadhus, Devadasis, Kanchanis, Tawaifs, and Nautch Girls—women who were once among the most accomplished artists, poets, musicians, patrons, and cultural icons of their age. Many enjoyed wealth, education, and social influence, supported temples and religious institutions, shaped artistic traditions, and moved comfortably within royal courts.Yet, with changing political and colonial attitudes, these women found themselves increasingly stigmatized, their identities reduced to stereotypes and their contributions pushed to the margins of history. We will trace this remarkable transformation and explore how many eventually carried their artistic legacy into the emerging world of Indian cinema, becoming singers, actors, dancers, and pioneers of the film industry.Through stories of power, performance, patronage, and prejudice, this walk seeks to rediscover the women who helped shape India's cultural landscape but whose voices have often been forgotten.Join Darwesh Heritage Walks at Qudsia Bagh as we uncover the extraordinary lives of India's courtesans—the custodians of art, culture, and memory.

location.address: 118, Yamuna Marg, Prema Kunj, Civil Lines, New Delhi, Delhi, 110054, India

name: Toontooni's Table x Lallan Sweets
description: What if you could taste a bestseller? Toontooni's Table is bringing Lallan Sweets to life, course by course.Join us for an intimate 6-course lunch inspired by Srishti Chaudhary's beloved novel, followed by a meet & greet with the author herself. Every dish on the table tells a story. Come hungry, leave with a signed Collector's Edition.

location.address: 60, Block M, Greater Kailash II, Greater Kailash, New Delhi, Delhi 110048, India

name: Notes From The Modern Invention of Sexuality
description: Join us for a lecture that asks an unsettling question:What if modern society did not become obsessed with sex because it was finally liberated—but because it found new ways to make us confess?About the LectureWe tend to think of talking about sex as an act of freedom. The more openly we speak about our desires, identities, fantasies, and intimacies, the more liberated we become.Michel Foucault was deeply suspicious of this story.In The History of Sexuality, he makes a strange claim: modern societies did not develop through the repression of sex, but through an ever-expanding demand that people speak about it. What began as a religious obligation to confess gradually spread into schools, hospitals, psychiatric institutions, government bureaucracies, and eventually into everyday life itself.The result was not simply greater knowledge about sexuality. It was the creation of sexuality as a category through which people could be classified, monitored, governed, and understood.Drawing on Foucault's account of confession, power, and biopolitics, this lecture explores:How sexuality became a modern object of knowledgeWhy institutions are invested in making intimate life legibleWhat confession has to do with governance and social controlHow reality television, social media, dating apps, and digital platforms transform self-disclosure into a cultural normWhy every search, swipe, message, and online interaction increasingly resembles a confession to a machineAt a moment when we are constantly encouraged to "be ourselves," "share our truth," and "tell our story," this lecture asks a provocative question: when we finally believe we are expressing our most authentic selves, who is listening—and what are they doing with what we reveal?About the SpeakerDr. Ananya Dasgupta is a scholar of literature, film, philosophy, and psychoanalysis. She studied English Literature at Jadavpur University before completing her doctoral research through IIT Bombay and Monash University, Melbourne.Her work explores sexuality and publicness, feminist engagements with law, and the relationship between politics and affect in contemporary culture. Her doctoral research examined figures of extra-legality in Hindi cinema. Her public writing has appeared in Scroll, ASAP Art, and The Ladies Finger.She currently teaches at Ashoka University, where she continues to explore questions of desire, power, selfhood, and the strange ways institutions teach us to speak about ourselves.Your ticket includes a 250/- coupon redeemable on F&B at the venue. name: Among Behemoths: On Tigers, Territory, And People
description: Join us for a lecture that challenges one of the most enduring myths of the natural world:That tigers belong somewhere far away from us.About the LectureWhen we think of tigers, we often imagine a solitary predator lurking deep inside untouched forests — a powerful but distant creature whose world exists entirely apart from our own.Much of modern conservation has been built around this image.But the reality is far more complicated.Across India, tigers move through landscapes shaped not only by forests, but by roads, farms, villages, canals, and human lives. Their stories are intertwined with ours in ways that are often overlooked, revealing a world where conflict and coexistence exist side by side.Drawing on years of fieldwork and a lifetime spent exploring India's forests, this lecture looks beyond the mythology of the tiger to understand the animal itself — its behaviour, its adaptability, and its relationship with the people who share its territory.The session explores:Why many popular assumptions about tigers are misleadingHow tiger behaviour differs from the image of the relentless apex predatorThe changing meaning of "wild" in an increasingly human-shaped worldHow communities and tigers negotiate shared landscapesWhat conservation looks like beyond the idea of keeping nature separate from peopleMoving between ecology, anthropology, conservation, and storytelling, the lecture examines what happens when we stop thinking of wilderness as a place apart and begin seeing it as something deeply entangled with human life.At its core, this is a story about adaptation — not only of tigers, but of all of us.About the SpeakerRudraksha Rishi Mitra is a social innovator, researcher, and founder of Canopy Wild, an immersive wildlife travel and education company dedicated to deepening public engagement with conservation and the natural world.A graduate of the London School of Economics in International Social and Public Policy, he has worked extensively across public policy, sustainable development, and social entrepreneurship. In 2022, he was selected as an Unleash Global Talent, recognising him among the world's leading young social innovators.His research has focused on conservation policy and human–tiger interactions in the Terai Arc Landscape, combining ethnographic methods with ecological inquiry. Over the years, he has explored and documented tiger landscapes across India, including the Nilgiri Biosphere, Annamalais, Terai Arc, and the Dooars.Having spent much of his life among forests and wildlife habitats, he is particularly interested in the ways people and large carnivores continue to share space in a rapidly changing world.Ticket IncludesYour ticket includes a 500/- coupon for F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Mode of transport - Ate glasshouse
description: Mode of Transport is the final stop of CTRLlab's Summer Coffee Crawl—an evening dedicated to journeys, movement, and the stories that connect people, places, and coffee across distances.As the series concludes, we turn our attention to the paths we travel: the routes coffee takes from farm to cup, the roads that lead us to new experiences, and the moments of movement that quietly shape our lives.Coffee Anchor follows coffee's remarkable journey from origin to cup, tracing the growers, traders, roasters, cafés, and communities that bring each bean across regions and continents before it reaches your hands. Together, we'll explore the networks, craftsmanship, and human stories that make coffee one of the world's most travelled beverages.Social Move invites guests to share stories from the road—memorable journeys, favourite commutes, unexpected detours, travel rituals, and the moments that altered our course. From everyday routes to life-changing adventures, we'll reflect on how movement often becomes the backdrop for our most meaningful memories.Hosted in an intimate setting designed for conversation and connection, the evening offers space to slow down, exchange stories, and appreciate the journeys—both ordinary and extraordinary—that bring us together.Summer Coffee Crawl 2025Stop 1 — Summer in the City14 June | Edit5, Sector 64, GurugramStop 2 — Coffee & Confessions21 June | Alayna Café, Shahpur JatStop 3 — Mode of Transport28 June | ATE Glasshouse, Lodhi RoadTickets- Single stop pass: Access to the event at that particular stop of the crawl- Three stop pass: Access to all 3 stops of the crawl

name: In Her Interest by Zerodha - Mutual Funds 101, Delhi
description: Women have a complicated relationship with money. And it starts early. Many families shield their daughters from anything related to ‘finance’, but this well-meaning protection ends up being a disservice. For when these young women step into the real world, they lack the confidence to make financial decisions - not because they’re incapable, but because they were never given information on budgeting, savings, and investments. Later in life, many continue to rely on their partners for investment decisions. This dependency on others will only stop when they have the right information and toolkit to take back control. That’s where we come in.We come back with our second 'In Her Interest'  session on 4th July in Delhi and will focus on one core idea:Mutual Funds 101 - all you need to know.﻿The session will be led by two amazing experts who truly get it.Shilpa Bhaskar GoleCertified Financial Planner® (CFP®) | Founder NerdyBird Wealth Advisory LLP - a Non-Individual SEBI Registered Investment Adviser (RIA)Neha SinghFounder at Womoneysta | Certified Financial Planner | SEBI-registered Once you buy a ticket, you also get to send a question that the speakers will address during the session.Note: Each session is theme-based. This session is about understanding Mutual Funds - we are not talking stocks and shares.
